As AI-driven power demand for data centers accelerates, grid operators are exploring whether large loads can do more than consume electricity – and instead become a source of flexibility when the power system is under stress. title: Germany Will Buy US-Made Tomahawk Missiles to Close Crucial Gap
summary: Germany agreed with the US to purchase long-range, subsonic Tomahawk cruise missile to close a dangerous gap with Russia — although numbers and delivery timelines are unclear, and Europe is developing such missiles itself.

title: New World Angers Rich Hong Kongers With High-End Private Club’s Revamp
summary: The millionaire members of the elite Town Club are upset with the billionaire Cheng family’s businesses, which own the venue.

title: Barclays’ Cau Says Stocks Can Look Past Middle East Volatility
summary: Investors should brace for more Middle East turmoil but resist the temptation to make major portfolio shifts as earnings growth remains the key driver for markets, according to Barclays Plc’s head of European equity strategy.

title: Chipmakers Fuel Gains in Stocks, Oil Fluctuates: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks climbed and oil fluctuated as investors speculated that tensions between the US and Iran will remain contained. Chipmakers rallied across regions.

title: Turkey’s Vestel Hires Advisers for Dollar Bond Restructuring
summary: Turkey’s Vestel Elektronik has hired advisers Houlihan Lokey for restructuring talks with bondholders, at the same time as the struggling electronics manufacturer renegotiates loans with local banks.

title: Copper Rallies as Risk Assets Shrug Off More US Strikes on Iran
summary: Copper jumped during a broad rally for industrial metals as investors shrugged off a fresh round of US strikes on Iran to track broader gains in risk assets.

title: Fuel Prices Show Inflation Is Still a Risk for Markets
summary: While oil prices have fallen, the cost of refined products like gasoline and diesel are proving to be much stickier.

title: Rain-Soaked China Braces For 'Gargantuan' Typhoon
summary: China is facing more torrential rain and flooding this weekend from a massive typhoon tracking toward the country’s east coast, following recent severe storms that led to deaths and crop damage. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India’s Wartime Buying Spree Leaves Behind a Cooking Gas Surplus
summary: India’s frenzied efforts to counter disruptions in cooking gas supplies over the past months, while cargoes remained trapped in the Persian Gulf, has left state-run fuel retailers with the opposite problem — more than they can sell.

title: Nigerian Stocks Vault Past Korea’s Kospi to Claim World’s Top Returns
summary: Nigerian equities have overtaken South Korea’s to hand investors the highest dollar-based returns this year, as souring sentiment on artificial-intelligence stocks pushes the Asian nation’s world-beating rally into bear territory.

title: Iran, US Trade Attacks For Second Day, SK Hynix To Set US Listing Price | The Pulse 7/9/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Emmanuel Cau, Barclays Head of European Equity Strategy; Francisco Blanch, Bank of America Head of Global Commodities and Derivatives Research; Judy Ganes, J Ganes Consulting President  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Jupiter Fund Cut Treasuries to Zero as Inflation Risks Rise
summary: Jupiter Asset Management has cut US Treasury holdings to zero in one of its main bond funds, buying European government notes and adding to its hefty emerging-markets position instead.

title: Hormuz Shipping Grinds to Near Halt After US, Iran Strikes
summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz came to a near standstill on Thursday after the US struck Iran for a second straight day, as a fragile truce between the two sides looked increasingly shaky.

title: Serbia Holds Rates as Election Welfare Plans Signal Price Risks
summary: Serbia kept borrowing costs unchanged for a record 22nd month as a government plan for cash handouts before this year’s general election may add to inflation pressures.

title: Erste Shares to Rally 30% on Poland Growth, Goldman Sachs Says
summary: Erste Group Bank AG shares are set to rally 30%, driven by growth from the lender’s expansion in Poland, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

title: Lenders Seek $700 Million After Japanese Firm’s Collapse
summary: Creditors across Japan are trying to claw back more than $700 million from a payments firm suspected to have faked its financial statement for at least 20 years and abruptly collapsed into bankruptcy this week.

title: US, Iran Trade Airstrikes as Fears Grow of a Return to War
summary: The US military struck Iran for a second day and Tehran retaliated against American allies in the Persian Gulf, raising fears of a return to war after little progress in efforts to secure a diplomatic outcome.

title: Barclays Readies $875 Million Debt Sale for Buyout of Senior Plc
summary: Barclays Plc is preparing to sell about $875 million of debt financing used to fund a takeover of UK aerospace supplier Senior Plc.

title: Oil Rally Cools as Traders Take Stock of Latest US-Iran Fighting
summary: Oil eased from its biggest gain since May as traders assessed the outlook for Middle Eastern crude supplies after fresh hostilities between the US and Iran.

title: Defense Valuations Yet to Peak as Orders Surge, BNP Banker Says
summary: European defense sector valuations still have plenty of upside, with government pledges to boost military spending now translating into orders, according to a top banker at BNP Paribas SA.

title: US Strikes Iran A Second Day; AI Rotation Trade on Watch | The Asia Trade 7/9/2026
summary:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Paul Allen, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.

UPDATE: Bain says a special-purpose investment vehicle it set up for SK Hynix still holds a 14% stake in Kioxia. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Apple Supplier Luxshare Drops After $3 Billion Hong Kong Debut
summary: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. fell in its Hong Kong trading debut on Thursday after raising HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion) in the city’s biggest listing this year so far.

title: The PWHL is Just Getting Started With Hilary Knight
summary: In this episode of The Deal, Hilary details for Jason and Alex the process of starting a league where players were partners from the jump and why Mark Walter and Billie Jean King were the right people to work with. She also explains how the momentum from this year’s Olympics bled into the PWHL season and why Heated Rivalry is one of the best things to ever happen to hockey. Finally, she shares her hopes for her post-playing career (spoiler alert! she wants to own a team) and the responsibility of being the face of her sport. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BlackRock’s Jewell Sees AI Capex Stable for Two to Three Years
summary: Commitments for AI spending should sustain the investment theme around the technology for two to three years, even as tech giants gradually turn cash-flow negative and start raising debt to fund their buildouts, according to BlackRock Inc.’s Helen Jewell.

title: Hugo Boss Board Rejects Frasers Bid as Not Reflecting Potential
summary: Hugo Boss AG recommended shareholders reject Frasers Group Plc’s takeover offer, saying the bid fails to capture the German fashion company’s long-term value.

title: UK’s Capita Sinks on Profit Hit From Pension Contract Failures
summary: Capita Plc tumbled after it said profit and cash flow will be hit by its failure to deliver on a contract to manage Britain’s civil service pension plan.

title: Oil Fluctuates After Second Day of US Strikes on Iran
summary: Oil swung as traders assessed the outlook for Middle Eastern crude supplies after US forces conducted a second day of strikes against Iran. Vessel-tracking data on Thursday showed a drop-off in transits through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China's Tech Stack Outshines its Economy: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Thailand and Malaysia Reach Deal to End Seafood Trade Spat
summary: Thailand and Malaysia have resolved a seafood trade dispute, paving the way for fishery trade between the two countries to return to normal soon, their leaders said.

title: How South Korea's SK Hynix Became Central to the AI Boom
summary:
SK Hynix may not be a household name, but the South Korean memory chipmaker is one of the top names in the hottest corner of the stock market.

Ahead of its US listing, Winnie Hsu looks at how the once-embattled company became a tech darling. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aston Martin’s Creditors Band Together Amid Debt Rout
summary: A group of creditors to Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc has signed a cooperation pact as mounting losses raise concerns among bondholders.
